name: Aldeacentenera from Castillo de Cabañas – circular through Geoparque Villuercas-Ibores-Jara
name_original: Aldeacentenera desde Castillo de Cabañas – circular por Geoparque Villuercas-Ibores-Jara
region_intro: Touring cycling around Cabañas Del Castillo, located within the UNESCO Global Geopark Villuercas-Ibores-Jara, offers routes through varied terrain. The region is characterized by the Villuercas mountains, featuring massive rock formations, deep gorges, and towering cliffs. Cyclists will encounter Mediterranean and Atlantic forests, including dehesas, and can follow the Almonte river. This area provides a unique geological backdrop for outdoor exploration. Best touring cycling routes around Cabañas Del Castillo The most popular touring cycling route is Aldeacentenera from Castillo de Cabañas – circular through Geoparque Villuercas-Ibores-Jara, a 29.8 miles (47.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 32 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers a circular journey through the geopark, passing by the historic Castillo de Cabañas. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Pico Villuercas Summit – Humilladero Pass loop from Ermita del Humilladero, a difficult 13.7 miles (22.1 km) path. This route includes ascents to the Pico Villuercas Summit and traverses the Humilladero Pass, providing views of the surrounding mountain landscape. Local touring cyclists also love the Guadalupe Viaduct – Pico Villuercas Summit loop from El Manzano, a 35.5 miles (57.1 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with significant elevation changes, often completed in about 4 hours 19 minutes. Touring cycling around Cabañas Del Castillo is defined by the rugged Villuercas mountains, ancient rock formations, and diverse forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, with a focus on challenging routes. The routes in Cabañas Del Castillo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 190 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cabañas Del Castillo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Cabañas Del Castillo?
A: There are 17 touring cycling routes available around Cabañas Del Castillo. These routes offer a variety of experiences through the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Cabañas Del Castillo?
A: The region's Mediterranean climate makes spring and autumn ideal for touring cycling. During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the natural environment, with its Mediterranean and Atlantic forests, is particularly vibrant.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Cabañas Del Castillo?
A: You can expect varied and often challenging terrain. The routes traverse the rugged Villuercas mountains, featuring massive rock formations, deep gorges, and towering cliffs. While some sections may be paved, many routes include significant ascents and descents, requiring good fitness.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes for beginners in Cabañas Del Castillo?
A: The touring cycling routes around Cabañas Del Castillo are generally considered challenging, with 14 out of 17 routes rated as difficult and 3 as moderate. There are currently no routes specifically categorized as easy for touring cyclists in this area.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120692630/aldeacentenera-from-castillo-de-cabanas-circular-through-geoparque-villuercas-ibores-jara" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aldeacentenera from Castillo de Cabañas – circular through Geoparque Villuercas-Ibores-Jara&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a journey through the geopark.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see while cycling around Cabañas Del Castillo?
A: The region is rich in history. You can visit the ruins of the medieval Castle of Cabañas del Castillo, which offers panoramic views. Further afield, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5717374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Monastery of Santa María de Guadalupe&lt;/a&gt;, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3847287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalupe Viaduct&lt;/a&gt; are significant points of interest that can be incorporated into longer routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38160254" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalupe Viaduct – View of Guadalupe loop from Navezuelas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or viewpoints along the touring cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3406495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Villuercas Summit&lt;/a&gt; offers impressive views, as does the Mirador de Cabañas del Castillo, known for its scenic vistas of the geopark. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6042271" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cancho del Fresno Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4461596" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Chiquita Cave&lt;/a&gt;. The Almonte river and its gorge also provide scenic beauty, as seen on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20133861" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Ruecas Gorge – Bridge Over the Almonte River loop from Cañamero&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the cycling routes in Cabañas Del Castillo?
A: Public transport options directly to the starting points of specific cycling routes in Cabañas Del Castillo may be limited due to the rural nature of the region. It is advisable to check local bus schedules or consider driving to the area.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Cabañas Del Castillo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the dramatic landscapes, the challenging ascents to viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3406495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Villuercas Summit&lt;/a&gt;, and the unique geological formations within the UNESCO Global Geopark Villuercas-Ibores-Jara.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Villuercas mountains?
A: Many routes provide stunning views of the Villuercas mountains. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20133807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Villuercas Summit – Humilladero Pass loop from Ermita del Humilladero&lt;/a&gt; specifically includes ascents to the summit, offering expansive mountain vistas.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through diverse forest types?
A: Yes, the region is known for its blend of Mediterranean and Atlantic forests. Routes often traverse areas with dehesas, oaks, and chestnuts, providing a rich and varied ecosystem to cycle through. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120692630/aldeacentenera-from-castillo-de-cabanas-circular-through-geoparque-villuercas-ibores-jara" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aldeacentenera from Castillo de Cabañas – circular through Geoparque Villuercas-Ibores-Jara&lt;/a&gt; is a good example of a route that showcases the geopark's diverse natural environment.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the area around Guadalupe?
A: Yes, some routes extend towards the Guadalupe area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38160588" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Guadalupe – Guadalupe Viaduct loop from Navalvillar de Ibor&lt;/a&gt; is a longer route that allows you to explore the mountainous terrain and offers views towards Guadalupe and its notable viaduct.
tips: A small bridge over the Almonte river. The bridge is the beginning of the ascent to Cabañas del Castillo. Spectacular views of the massive rock formation and gorge above.
Located at the foot of the Sierra de las Villuercas, is the small town of Aldeacentenera. Gateway to the UNESCO Sierra de las Villuercas Biosphere Reserve.

Cabañas del Castillo is a spectacular place located within the Villuercas-Ibores-Jara Geopark, dominated by high rock formations and gorges. The Cabañas castle is of Muslim origin but was later occupied by the Knights of Trujillo and Calatrava. A short ride off the bike, but highly recommended for seeing incredible views between the Sierra de la Ortijuela and Sierra del Alcornocal and the gorge below curved by the Almonte River.
